---
title: "LRP-CA-004: Concurrent conditional associations — skills → taught expressive vocabulary (per wave)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP-CA-004 extends the **concurrent conditional-associations** family (#312,
descriptive-association workstream #314) to **taught expressive vocabulary** (block 1,
`TE`) as the focal outcome. At each timepoint (t1–t4) it fits a between-child
Beta-Binomial regression of taught expressive vocabulary's level on the standardised
same-wave logits of the other core skills — word reading (`W`), letter sounds (`L`),
blending (`B`), taught receptive vocabulary (`TR`), and receptive (`R`) and expressive
(`E`) vocabulary — plus age and a group nuisance term. The four waves are fitted
separately and reported side by side.

The family's core skill set is {`W`, `L`, `B`, `TR`, `TE`, `R`, `E`}: each model
conditions its focal outcome on the remaining six, so alongside `lrp-rli-ca-001`
(word reading) and `lrp-rli-ca-002` (letter sounds) the models describe the
conditional joint distribution of the same measure set from different sides. The
taught receptive companion `TR` stays in the predictor set — the two taught tests are
strongly correlated, but the regularising priors handle the collinearity and the
adjusted-vs-bivariate gap is itself informative. All children are on the intervention
from t2, so these describe the conditional joint distribution of the skill measures
within a treated system.

::: {.callout-note title="Estimand: descriptive association (no causal warrant)"}
Every coefficient is an **adjusted association**. Conditioning on contemporaneous
(post-treatment) skill levels is intentional and licensed *because nothing here is read
causally*. Read the results with the Table-2-fallacy, regression-dilution and
collinearity-shrinkage caveats set out in the Results section.
:::

## Design

- **Four separate cross-sectional fits** (one row per child per wave), reported side
  by side — not one stacked model with a child random intercept, which would tilt the
  coefficients toward a within-child quantity and change the estimand (issue #312
  decision). The best-powered wave carries the standard convergence gate / posterior
  diagnostics below; the other waves and all bivariate fits are sub-fits with their own
  convergence flags in the tables.
- **Predictors** enter as standardised same-wave logits with regularising
  `Normal(0, 0.3)` slopes (essential at n ≈ 53 with a collinear predictor cluster).
  Floored measures (`P`, `N`) are excluded as predictors.
- **Group** is included only as a non-interpretable nuisance term (absorbs arm
  composition at each wave); it is not reported as an association.
- **Missing predictor values** are mean-imputed (0 on the standardised scale); a
  predictor's realised variance shrinks with its missingness, biasing that coefficient
  toward zero — flagged, as in the horseshoe level model. Rows missing the focal
  *outcome* are dropped.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_concurrent.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
